title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
Regular expressions (regex) are patterns used to match, search, and manipulate text.

Creating a regex:
• Literal: /pattern/flags
• Constructor: new RegExp('pattern', 'flags')

Flags:
• g — global (find all matches)
• i — case-insensitive
• m — multiline

Essential methods:
• regex.test(str) — returns true/false
• str.match(regex) — returns matches
• str.replace(regex, replacement) — replace matches
• str.split(regex) — split by pattern

Common patterns:
• \d — digit, \w — word character, \s — whitespace
• ^ — start, $ — end, . — any character
• + — one or more, * — zero or more, ? — zero or one
• {n,m} — between n and m occurrences
• [] — character class, () — group, | — or
